title: The Hackathon Story
description: The project story from start to finish — how a single-lane test track became a thousand-lane highway for financial contract evaluation, told through the car factory analogy.
blurb: project story, objectives, timeline, decisions, challenges, and outcomes

title: Background — GPU Computing
description: What a GPU is, how it differs from a CPU, and why GPU architecture is well-suited for large-scale financial contract evaluation using ILGPU.
blurb: GPU computing fundamentals, CPU vs GPU architecture, and the ILGPU framework

title: ACTUS Financial Contracts
description: The ACTUS standard as implemented in this project — contract types, the event system, state transitions, and risk factor handling.
blurb: ACTUS contract standard, PAM contract type, events, and risk factors

title: Technical Documentation
description: Full implementation reference — core engine architecture, PAM contract processor, state machine, financial conventions, schedule generation, GPU acceleration, demos, and testing.
blurb: implementation details, code architecture, GPU acceleration, and testing
features: Core Engine — PAM Contract, State Machine, Conventions
GPU Acceleration — Data Structures, Kernels, Executors

title: ACTUS Insurance Extensions
description: How the ACTUS framework was extended to model insurance contracts — life insurance projection, Markov state transitions, and a domain-specific language for product rules.
blurb: life insurance modeling, Markov transitions, and DSL for product rules

title: Shared Reference
description: Cross-cutting reference material — documentation navigation map and terminology glossary used across all sections.
blurb: navigation map, terminology glossary, and cross-references
